claim: Bearish consensus on AI is itself becoming crowded, implying positioning asymmetry favors upside on 3-12 month horizon. BofA GFS shows AI/tech allocation declining to roughly neutral, NASDAQ put/call at 1.3x, NVDA short interest up to 3.5%.
needs: AI-specific positioning data (AI ETF flows, company-level aggregated shorts) rather than broad NASDAQ positioning data which conflates rate views and tariff risk with AI-specific sentiment

claim: BLS multifactor productivity measurement is the weakest link, potentially understating AI impact by 0.1-0.5pp annually due to 18-24 month lag, establishment survey weights, and hedonic adjustment gaps.
needs: Updated BLS methodology review specific to AI-era output; replication of Nordhaus digital services mismeasurement estimate with current data; Boskin Commission-style recalibration study

claim: Agent AI capabilities may compress GPT diffusion lag from historical 5-15 years to 4-12 years with bimodal distribution across software-native vs regulated/physical sectors.
needs: Enterprise-scale deployment data rather than lab demonstrations (SWE-bench); n>0 examples of agent AI transforming 50,000+ person enterprise workflows; regulatory friction timeline estimates from regulated industries

claim: China's AI trajectory is the highest-variance binary swing factor for the dollar cycle with $200-400B annual capital flow differential between scenarios.
needs: Empirically calibrated probability distribution rather than the current 25/50/25 assumption; more granular TIC data separating China-to-US tech redirected flows from general portfolio reallocation

claim: Services-exporting EM economies (India IT ~$200B/6% GDP, Philippines BPO ~$33-35B/8% GDP) face 5-15 year structural current account erosion from AI automation.
needs: Better calibrated automation timeline estimates than McKinsey's historically poor forecasts; NDF trade construction with appropriate tenor matching the 5-25 year structural thesis rather than 2-3 year standard tenors

claim: S&P 493 AI narrative premium of ~1-2 P/E turns ($200-800B market cap) driven by earnings call AI mention frequency rather than genuine AI adoption.
needs: Controlled study separating AI mention frequency from actual AI adoption and margin improvement to establish causal direction; more precise magnitude estimate beyond the illustrative $500-800B range

claim: Political economy of white-collar displacement creates 25-40% probability of meaningful AI-specific labor regulation in 2+ G7 economies by 2028.
needs: Formally specified sample of major displacement episodes for base rate calculation; clearer transmission mechanism from political influence to specific regulatory outcomes; precedent analysis of white-collar displacement regulatory responses

claim: Fiscal consolidation is paradoxically dollar-negative through the Kalecki profit channel: reducing deficit shrinks profit floor, reduces equity earnings, reduces capital inflows, weakens capex dollar.
needs: Empirical validation from at least one historical episode where this specific mechanism dominated; the logic is sound within the framework but has never been tested as the dominant FX transmission channel
